evals.expected_output: Skill identifies no AGENTS.md exists. It starts Phase 0 survey (exactly 3 questions in order: commit policy, maturity, testing). Then runs analyze-all (which also detects CI/CD automatically), scaffolds with discovered parameters, wires CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md, and lints.
expectations: Skill asks exactly 3 AskUserQuestion prompts in Phase 0 (Commit, Maturity, Testing) — never CI/CD
Skill runs 'analyze-all' script to discover toolchain, structure, and CI provider
Skill uses 'scaffold-agents-md' with correct marker values from Phase 0, with ci= sourced from auto-detection
Skill wires CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md as one-line redirect stubs
Skill runs 'lint-agents-md' and fixes FAIL-level issues

evals.expected_output: Skill enters Audit Mode. It runs 'lint-agents-md' on the existing file and reports issues found. It does not attempt to regenerate or survey unless requested.
expectations: Skill does NOT run Phase 0 survey
Skill reports issues found in the lint output

evals.prompt: Refresh our AGENTS.md. It already has a marker: <!-- codebase-init:hard-rules v1 commit=strict maturity=production testing=always ci=github-actions -->
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the v1 marker and skips Phase 0 survey. It proceeds directly to Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 drafting (preserving existing sections), and Phase 3 validation.
expectations: Skill detects the v1 marker in AGENTS.md
Skill runs analyze-all and proceeds with regeneration
Skill preserves existing non-generated sections during regeneration

evals.prompt: Add AGENTS.md but I don't want to answer any policy questions right now.
evals.expected_output: Skill starts Phase 0 survey. When user cancels or refuses, skill halts immediately without writing any files or proceeding to Phase 1.
expectations: Skill stops execution after user cancels a Phase 0 question
